key_points: The lesson 'Kathmandu' is written by Vikram Seth
It is a travelogue describing the author's visit to Kathmandu
The author visits the Pashupatinath temple and Boudhanath stupa
Pashupatinath temple is crowded and chaotic
Boudhanath stupa has a peaceful and calm atmosphere
characters: Narrator – The author Vikram Seth who describes his travel experience
Flute seller – A street vendor selling bamboo flutes
Priests and devotees – People performing rituals at Pashupatinath temple
themes: Religious traditions of Hinduism and Buddhism
Power of music in connecting people
literary_devices: Imagery – Descriptions of temples, streets and rituals
Simile – Flutes compared to the quills of a porcupine
exam_tips: Remember the difference between Pashupatinath temple and Boudhanath stupa
Mention the flute seller and the symbolism of flute music
Focus on cultural diversity and religious traditions
